prompt:
Overall house check at 03:30. Current observed state:
Livingroom light off, fan 0%, PIR motion detected, temperature 25.0C.
Hallway left light off, right light off, fan 0%, main door 50% (half open), PIR motion detected, temperature 25.3C.
Bedroom room light off, bed light off, balcony light off, fan 25%, curtain 0%, lock locked, pump off, PIR motion detected, temperature 26.0C, outside temperature 24.5C, moisture 50%.
eval_note: Correct if the agent recognizes motion in ALL rooms at 3:30AM with the main door half-open as a critical security threat. It should immediately close the door, turn on all lights for maximum visibility and deterrence, and issue an urgent alert. Bedroom lock should stay locked since it already is.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant S as ScheduleSession
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
S->>A: 3:30AM motion everywhere with door open
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=1 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=true
A->>C: set espID=3 led1=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>S: URGENT security alert - motion in all rooms with door open at 3:30AM

else no motion

else no rain

It is 2026-04-07 05:45:00, too early and have not reached any events or execution yet.
Have a general check of the house appliances system and compare it with the initial settings below:
{
  "livingroom": {"led1": false, "motor1": 0},
  "hallway": {"led1": false, "led2": false, "motor1": 0, "motor2": 0},
  "bedroom": {"led1": false, "led2": false, "led3": false, "motor1": 0, "motor2": 0, "servo": true, "pump": false}
}
Please provide your suggestions or actions to ensure the appliances are set up correctly.
Current observed state:
Livingroom light on, fan 25%.
Hallway left light off, right light on, fan 10%, main door 40% (partially open).
Bedroom room light off, bed light on, balcony light off, fan 0%, curtain 30%, lock unlocked, pump on.
eval_note: Correct if the agent fixes all mismatches: living room light off and fan to 0, hallway right light off and fan to 0 and door to 0, bedroom bed light off and curtain to 0 and lock to locked and pump off. Multiple mismatches across all rooms.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant S as ScheduleSession
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
S->>A: Init check with many mismatches
A->>C: set espID=1 led1=false
A->>C: set espID=1 motor1=0
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=false
A->>C: set espID=2 motor1=0
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=3 led2=false
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=3 servo=true
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=false
C-->>A: success
A-->>S: report all mismatches corrected to init state

prompt: What notes do I have for tomorrow? If there are any, read them all out to me.
eval_note: Correct if the agent first uses get_current_datetime to determine today, calculates tomorrow's date, then check_note and read_note for that date. Must use time tool first to know what tomorrow is.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant T as TimeTool
participant N as NoteTool
U->>A: Read tomorrow's notes
A->>T: get_current_datetime()
T-->>A: current date
A->>N: check_note(tomorrow_date)
N-->>A: count/ids
A->>N: read_note(date=tomorrow_date)
N-->>A: note contents
A-->>U: present all notes for tomorrow

prompt: Add a note for 2026-04-10 saying 'Dentist appointment at 14:00' and another separate note for the same day saying 'Pick up dry cleaning after dentist'. Then confirm both were added.
eval_note: Correct if the agent creates two separate notes with different content for the same date. Two add_note calls needed since the notes have different content.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant N as NoteTool
U->>A: Add two separate notes for same date
A->>N: add_note(dentist appointment, [2026-04-10])
N-->>A: note 1 created
A->>N: add_note(pick up dry cleaning, [2026-04-10])
N-->>A: note 2 created
A-->>U: confirm both notes added for 2026-04-10

prompt: Open the main door wide open for fresh air but also keep the house completely secure from intruders.
eval_note: Correct if the agent identifies the contradiction: a wide-open door is incompatible with complete security. It should suggest a compromise like partial opening (30-50%) or explain the trade-off and ask for priority.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: Open door wide but keep secure
A-->>U: explain contradiction, suggest partial opening or ask for priority

prompt: Turn on all the bedroom lights and also make the room pitch dark for sleeping.
eval_note: Correct if the agent identifies the contradiction between turning on all lights and wanting pitch darkness. It should suggest keeping lights off for sleep or ask which the user actually wants.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: All lights on but pitch dark
A-->>U: identify contradiction, ask whether user wants lights for visibility first or darkness for sleep

prompt: Set the livingroom fan to -10%.
eval_note: Correct if the agent rejects the negative percentage as invalid (valid range is 0-100) and asks for a valid value.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: Set fan to -10%
A-->>U: reject negative value, request valid 0-100 range

prompt: Turn the balcony light to 50%.
eval_note: Correct if the agent explains that the balcony light (led3) is a boolean actuator (on/off only) and does not support dimming. It should ask whether the user wants it on or off.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: Set balcony light to 50%
A-->>U: explain balcony light is on/off only, ask for on or off

prompt: Set the light.
eval_note: Correct if the agent asks for clarification: which room's light and what action (on/off). The request is incomplete.

sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: Set the light
A-->>U: ask which room and what action (on or off)

prompt: Change the fan.
eval_note: Correct if the agent asks for clarification: which room's fan and what value/action. The request is too vague to act on.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: Change the fan
A-->>U: ask which room fan and desired setting

prompt: Read the moisture sensor. If it's below 25%, water the bonsai and then read the moisture again after watering to confirm it changed. Report both readings.
eval_note: Correct if the agent reads moisture, conditionally waters, then reads moisture again. The second read verifies the pump effect. Three steps in total with conditional middle step.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Moisture check, conditional water, re-check
A->>C: get espID=3 mois
C-->>A: moisture value
alt moisture < 25
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=true
C-->>A: success
A->>C: get espID=3 mois
C-->>A: updated moisture
A-->>U: report both readings
else moisture >= 25
A-->>U: report moisture sufficient, no watering needed

prompt: Check if there is motion in any room. Give me a full occupancy report for the entire house.
eval_note: Correct if the agent reads all three PIR sensors and reports occupancy status for each room. No appliance changes expected.

sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Full house occupancy report
A->>C: get espID=1 pir
A->>C: get espID=2 pir
A->>C: get espID=3 pir
C-->>A: PIR values
A-->>U: report occupancy status for livingroom, hallway, and bedroom

prompt: What's the best indoor temperature for sleeping according to health experts?
eval_note: Correct if the agent uses web search or answers from knowledge (18-22C is commonly recommended). No appliance action needed unless the user asks for adjustment.

sequenceDiagram

participant A as Agent
participant W as WebSearchTool
U->>A: Best sleep temperature?
A->>W: search_and_read_web_link(best indoor temperature for sleeping)
W-->>A: health info
A-->>U: summarize recommended sleep temperature

sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant W as WebSearchTool

A-->>U: list chocolate cake ingredients

prompt: Get the current date and time. Search online for sunset time at my location today. If sunset is within 2 hours, turn on the hallway lights and livingroom light preemptively.
eval_note: Correct if the agent uses get_current_datetime, get_current_location, then web search for sunset time. Then conditionally turns on lights if sunset is within 2 hours. Time + location + web + conditional appliance.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant T as TimeTool
participant L as LocationTool
participant W as WebSearchTool
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Sunset-based preemptive lighting
A->>T: get_current_datetime()
T-->>A: current time
A->>L: get_current_location()
L-->>A: location
A->>W: search_and_read_web_link(sunset time at location today)
W-->>A: sunset time
alt sunset within 2 hours
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=true
A->>C: set espID=1 led1=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: report sunset soon, lights turned on
else sunset not soon
A-->>U: report sunset time, no action needed yet

prompt: What is the temperature inside and outside the bedroom? Also tell me the current weather forecast. Based on all that information, recommend whether I should open or close the curtain and adjust the fan.
eval_note: Correct if the agent reads bedroom temp and outside temp sensors, gets weather forecast (location + hourly forecast), and provides a recommendation. The agent may or may not execute — recommendation is the primary ask.

sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
participant L as LocationTool
participant W as WeatherTool
U->>A: Sensor + weather combined recommendation
A->>C: get espID=3 tem
A->>C: get espID=3 tem_out
C-->>A: temperature values
A->>L: get_current_location()
L-->>A: location
A->>W: get_hourly_forecast(location, today)
W-->>A: forecast
A-->>U: recommend curtain and fan settings based on all data

prompt:

participant A as Agent

prompt: Check the weather forecast for today. If it will be above 35°C at any point today, maximum all fans, close the curtain to block heat, and turn off the balcony light. Also add a note saying 'Extreme heat day — stay hydrated'. If under 35°C all day, just tell me the forecast.
eval_note: Correct if the agent gets location, gets forecast, checks for any hour above 35C, then conditionally sets fans to 100%, closes curtain, turns off balcony light, and adds a note. Must use location, weather, conditional appliance, and note tools.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant L as LocationTool
participant W as WeatherTool
participant C as ApplianceController
participant N as NoteTool
U->>A: Heat-wave conditional multi-action
A->>L: get_current_location()
L-->>A: location
A->>W: get_hourly_forecast(location, today)
W-->>A: forecast
alt any hour above 35C
A->>C: set espID=1 motor1=100
A->>C: set espID=2 motor1=100
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=100
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=3 led3=false
C-->>A: success
A->>N: add_note(extreme heat day, [today])
N-->>A: note created
A-->>U: report heat wave actions taken and note added
else under 35C all day
A-->>U: report forecast summary, no action needed
